text: Arabic Language Audio Comments by Prof. Mohamed Ashraf Madkour
➢ Web now widely used by business, government, individuals ➢ but Internet & Web are vulnerable ➢ have a variety of threats

Several approaches to providing Web security are possible. The various approaches that have been considered are similar in the services they provide and, to some extent, in the mechanisms that they use, but they differ with respect to their scope of applicability and their relative location within the TCP/IP protocol stack.
➢ transport layer security service ➢ originally developed by Netscape ➢ version 3 designed with public input ➢ subsequently became Internet standard known as TLS
➢ uses TCP to provide a reliable end-to-end service ➢ SSL has two layers of protocols
a transient, peer-to-peer, communications link • associated with one SSL session
an association between client & server • created by the Handshake Protocol • define a set of cryptographic parameters • may be shared by multiple SSL connections
using symmetric encryption with a shared secret key defined by Handshake Protocol
can use several encryption ciphers such as: AES, IDEA, RC2-40, DES-40, DES, 3DES, Fortezza, RC4-40, RC4-128
using a MAC with shared secret key • like HMAC but with different padding
➢ one of 3 SSL specific protocols which use the SSL
Record protocol ➢ a single message ➢ causes pending state to become current ➢ hence updating the cipher suite in use
➢ conveys SSL-related alerts to peer entity ➢ severity
fatal: unexpected message, bad record mac, decompression failure, handshake failure, illegal parameter
warning: close notify, no certificate, bad certificate, unsupported certificate, certificate revoked, certificate expired, certificate unknown
➢ compressed & encrypted like all SSL data

to negotiate encryption & MAC algorithms to negotiate cryptographic keys to be used ➢ comprises a series of messages in four phases Establish Security Capabilities Server Authentication and Key Exchange Client Authentication and Key Exchange Finish
a one-time 48-byte value • generated using secure key exchange (RSA / Diffie-Hellman) and then hashing information.
➢ generation of cryptographic parameters (6 secret values as follows)

client write MAC secret, a server write MAC secret, a client write key, a server write key, a client write IV, and a server write IV these 6 secrets are generated by hashing the master secret using a simple algorithm and a hash function.
➢ IETF standard RFC 2246 like SSLv3 ➢ with minor differences
• uses HMAC for MAC • a pseudo-random function expands secrets ▪ based on HMAC using SHA-1 or MD5
has additional alert codes • some changes in supported ciphers • changes in certificate types & negotiations • changes in cryptographic computations & padding
combination of HTTP & SSL/TLS to secure communications between browser & server • documented in RFC2818 • no fundamental change using either SSL or TLS
use https:// URL rather than http://
URL, document contents, form data, cookies, HTTP headers
➢ For HTTPS, the agent acting as the HTTP client also acts as the
have “Connection: close” in HTTP record • TLS level exchange close_notify alerts • can then close TCP connection • must handle TCP close before alert exchange sent or completed
➢ Have considered: • Web security • TLS / SSL protocol • HTTPS
